title: cymopterus acaulis var. higginsii
intro: Cymopterus acaulis var. higginsii, also known as Higgins' spring-parsley, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Higgins' spring-parsley include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs, and soothe digestive issues. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be made by steeping the roots or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
